layman_headline: Crypto markets are bleeding — patience is the smartest trade right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is selling off today, dragged down by a global tech/AI stock rout that's spilling into crypto. Bitcoin dropped below $63,000 and most altcoins are down 3-10%. Fear is rising but hasn't hit extreme panic levels yet. This means prices could still fall further before a real bounce opportunity appears.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines. Set price alerts for Ethereum around $1,700-$1,720 — that's where a stronger buying opportunity could emerge if the selloff continues.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling risk-adjusted entry right now. DEXE has an RSI of 85 — dangerously overbought and a fade candidate, not a buy. HYPE dropped 13% in a week but RSI is still 59, not yet oversold enough for a bounce trade. BONK is in a confirmed downtrend with bearish chart bias of -1.4 — catching a falling knife. ETH at RSI 35 is the most interesting but it's below all major moving averages with a neutral chart bias score of 0.0, and the broader market risk-off sentiment from the AI sector collapse hasn't stabilized. BTC dominance at 56.2% continues to suppress altcoin performance. The Fear & Greed index at 27 is improving from 25 but hasn't hit the extreme fear zone (sub-20) that historically marks the best swing entry points. Better to wait for RSI to dip below 30 or for a clear reversal candle pattern.
risk_factors: Global risk-off selling from AI/tech sector could accelerate, pushing BTC below $60K and dragging all alts further
BTC dominance at 56.2% means capital is flowing away from altcoins — any alt position fights the macro trend
Fear & Greed at 27 is 'fear' but not 'extreme fear' — historically the best entries come at sub-20 readings
watch_list: BTC holding $60,000 support — a break below would trigger cascading liquidations across alts
ETH RSI approaching 30 or a bullish reversal candle at $1,700 support — would signal entry opportunity
Fear & Greed index dropping to sub-20 extreme fear — historically the best contrarian buy zone
market_context: A global risk-off wave triggered by AI/semiconductor sector selloffs is dragging crypto lower. BTC is testing $62,800 with declining momentum, and altcoins are underperforming significantly as BTC dominance stays elevated at 56.2%.
context_banner: Markets are in a risk-off correction driven by tech contagion — no high-conviction swing setups exist until fear deepens or a reversal confirms.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory but hasn't crossed below 30, which historically marks higher-probability bounce entries. Our 80% win rate on RSI signals means waiting for a cleaner setup.

Multiple headlines confirm a global risk-off wave: 'chip rout goes global,' 'AI selloff spreads from stocks to crypto,' and 'Ether falls twice as hard as bitcoin' — indicating contagion risk is active and not yet contained.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) is improving from 25 but hasn't reached extreme fear territory. The trend is slightly improving but not enough to signal a contrarian buy.
'Bitcoin bottom countdown nears 50 days after BTC supply in loss passed 50%' — suggests we may be in a bottoming process but it's not yet complete, supporting patience over action.
'ETH derivatives reset and the next retail trade' and 'Finding good news in spite of bad headlines' suggest institutional analysts see opportunity forming but timing remains premature.
